data.title: Singapore Shares Climb 6.6%, Tracking Wall Street's RallyMarket Talk
data.description: Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index climbs 6.6% to 3617.89 in early trade, tracking Wall Street's rally overnight. President Trump's announcement of a 90-day pause in some of the U.S. reciprocal tariffs on the world has caused a "market that has been under extreme pressure for the past week to explode higher," Phillip Securities Research team says in a commentary. Among the best performers on the benchmark index, SATS Ltd. rises 8.5%, index heavyweight DBS adds 8.2%, and Mapletree Logistics Trust is 8.6% higher. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Oil prices fall as Trump escalates trade war with China
data.description:
Oil prices fell around 1% on Thursday as U.S. President Donald Trump ramped up a trade war with China, even as he announced a 90-day pause on tariffs aimed at other countries.

Brent futures fell 73 cents, 1.1%, to $64.73 a barrel by 01:08 a.m. GMT, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures lost 49 cents, or 0.8%, to $61.86.

The benchmarks had settled 4% higher on Wednesday after dropping as much as 7% during the session.

Trump raised the tariff rate for China to 125%, effective immediately, from the previously announced 104% tariff that had kicked off earlier on Wednesday.

China also announced an additional import levy on U.S. goods, imposing an 84% tariff from Thursday.

Meanwhile, U.S. crude inventories rose by 2.6 million barrels in the week to April 4, the Energy Information Administration said, nearly double the expectations in a Reuters poll for a 1.4-million-barrel rise.

The Keystone oil pipeline from Canada to the United States remained shut on Wednesday following an oil spill near Fort Ransom, North Dakota, while plans to return it to service were being evaluated, its operator South Bow said.

data.title: South Korean Shares Rebound in Thursday's Morning Trade on US' 90-Day Tariff Pause
data.description:
South Korean shares rebounded on Thursday morning, after Trump announced a 90-day tariff pause for most nations but further escalated the trade war with China, raising tariffs to 125% over Beijing's adjustment of retaliatory tariffs on Wednesday. He also proposed a 10% universal tariff as bilateral talks continued.

As of 9:23 am KST, Thursday, the Korea Composite Stock Price Index, or Kospi, was trading 114.32 points, or nearly 5% higher at 2,408.02 and the Kosdaq gained 28.16 points, or about 4.4%, at 671.55.

Shares of major market movers such as Samsung Electronics , SK Hynix , and Hyundai Motor , among others, gained 6%, over 12%, and nearly 6% respectively in recent trade.

Trump's announcement came a day after China imposed 84% retaliatory tariffs on US-made goods, starting April 10. The hike significantly increased from the East Asian country's previously announced 34% tariffs, after US' sweeping reciprocal tariffs on Chinese goods totaled at least 104%. However, Trump said that he would further raise tariffs on Chinese imports to 125%.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 9 PM ET
data.description:
Asian Markets Power Higher After Trump's About-Face on Tariffs

Asian stock markets powered higher in early trade, tracking overnight gains on Wall Street after President Trump's decision to pause steep tariffs on a swath of countries that had been put into effect just hours before.

Trump Transforms Tariff War Into High-Stakes Showdown With China

The clash locks the world's two largest economies in an extraordinary conflict without any immediate exits.

Trump Pauses 'Reciprocal' Tariffs, but Hits China Harder

The 10% global baseline levy for all imports will stay in effect.

On-Again Off-Again Tariffs Send Americans Rushing to Stock Up

Covid memories of shortages, price increases and supply-chain shocks are motivating Americans to load up on vinegar, tires, furniture.

Apple Stock Jumps on Tariff News, Hope for Exemption

President Donald Trump is raising China's tariff rate to 125% but also said he is considering excluding imports by some companies.

Trump's Tariff Pause 'Welcome Reprieve' For Global Economy, Canada PM Carney Says

Carney said the 90-day pause, and Trump's pledge to negotiate new trading arrangements with dozens of countries, "will likely result in a fundamental restructuring of the global trading system."

Nasdaq Soars to Best Day Since 2001 After Trump Pauses Some Tariffs

Stocks surged in a historic rally. The Nasdaq's 12% rise was its biggest move upward since Jan. 3, 2001.

Minneapolis Fed's Kashkari Says Tariffs Raise the Bar for Fed Interest-Rate Moves

The central bank's "first priority must be keeping long-run inflation expectations anchored," Kashkari says.

Fed Officials Flagged Risks of 'More Persistent' Inflation From Tariffs

Some policymakers at last month's meeting warned of difficult trade-offs that loom if inflation rises and hiring deteriorates.

Bond Market Calms as 10-Year Treasury Auction Sees 'Very Strong' Demand

Worries of a so-called busted sale had been rising.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 9 PM ET
data.description:
Constellation Brands to Divest Cheaper Wine Brands Amid Organizational Review

Alcohol heavyweight Constellation Brands will undergo a review of its organizational structuring that is expected to save the company more than $200 million by 2028.

EQT Stake Sale Values Swedish Software Developer IFS at Over $16 Billion

The roughly $3.29 billion deal gives Hg control of the company alongside EQT.

Accounting Firms Baker Tilly and Moss Adams Near Merger

Moss Adams would become partly owned by private equity through the deal, which would create a combined firm with more than $3 billion in annual revenue.

Walmart Confident About Tariff Resilience Despite Sales Volatility

Walmart reaffirmed its first-quarter sales guidance and widened its range for operating income growth to stay flexible on pricing as tariffs go into effect.

Delta Air Lines Pulls Guidance on Trump Tariff Uncertainty. Why Airline Stocks Are Rising.

Delta Air Lines scraps its full-year guidance Wednesday and warns 'growth has largely stalled' amid global economic uncertainty.

Remy Cointreau CEO to Leave Group

European drinks makers face waning demand after a boom in beverage consumption during the pandemic as well as uncertainty in key markets.

Harley-Davidson Director Resigns Citing 'Grave Concerns' About Company

Jared Dourdeville of the New York investment firm H Partners said the company had suffered from "cultural depletion."

WeightWatchers Prepares for Bankruptcy

The health and wellness company is preparing to file for bankruptcy in the coming months as part of a plan to hand control of the business to its creditors.

Pharma Stocks Slide Further After Trump Reiterates Tariff Threats on Drugs

Drugmakers have said they're still unclear about whether Trump will impose tariffs on finished products or on active pharmaceutical ingredients.

Volkswagen Vehicle Deliveries Rise Despite China Weakness

The automaker has been hit by intense competition in China that has seen rivals cut prices to win customers in its largest market.

data.title: Aussie Broadband Posts Higher Fiscal Q3 Total Connections, Reaffirms Guidance; Shares Rise 6%
data.description:
Aussie Broadband reported total broadband connections of 752,230 for the fiscal third quarter, up from 665,983 a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company reaffirmed its fiscal 2025 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization guidance of between AU$133 million and AU$138 million, the filing said.

Shares of the company rose 6% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: China Raises Tariffs on US Goods to 84% in Retaliatory Move
data.description:
China has raised tariffs on US imports to 84% from 34%, effective Thursday, April 10, in response to US President Donald Trump's 104% levy on Chinese goods.

The notice was issued by China's Office of the Tariff Commission of the State Council late on Wednesday after Trump slapped an additional 50% tariff on China earlier in the day.

The Commission described the US's tariff actions as "a mistake on top of a mistake."

Additionally, China has added six more US firms to its unreliable entity list and placed 12 US entities on its export control list.

China has also lodged a complaint against the US with the World Trade Organization, according to state-run Xinhua News Agency.

"I want to emphasize that there is no winner in a trade war, and that China does not want a trade war. But the Chinese government will by no means sit by when the legitimate rights and interests of its people are being hurt and deprived," an official of China's Ministry of Commerce said.

data.title: Oil Holds Advance After US Tariff Pause
data.description:
WTI crude oil futures traded around $62.5 per barrel on Thursday, holding onto a more than 4% rebound from the previous session as risk appetite improved following the US decision to pause higher tariffs for most countries.

On Wednesday, President Trump reduced import duties to 10% for a 90-day period to support ongoing trade negotiations, easing fears of a full-scale trade war that could trigger a global recession and dampen oil demand.

However, caution remains as China was excluded from the suspension, with its tariff rate surging to 125% effective immediately.

Adding to market concerns, OPEC+ recently agreed to increase production in May, exerting more supply to an already oversupplied market.

Meanwhile, the latest EIA report revealed a larger-than-expected draw in gasoline and distillate inventories, helping to offset a modest rise in crude stockpiles.

data.title: China Issues Warning on Travel to U.S.
data.description:
China issued a new travel advisory for the U.S. on Wednesday, citing the "deterioration of economic and trade relations" and the "security situation," amid escalating tensions between the two countries.

The advisory from the Chinese Ministry of Culture and Tourism came as both nations imposed fresh retaliatory tariffs.

President Trump announced on Truth Social that 125% tariffs on China would take effect "immediately" and introduced a temporary 10% tariff on other nations for 90 days.

While many countries are seeking talks to avoid a fallout, China has vowed to take "countermeasures" if needed.

The U.S. currently has China on a level two travel advisory, a downgrade from level three (“reconsider travel”) that followed a prisoner swap last November.

Brent crude oil futures traded around $65.5 per barrel on Thursday, holding onto a more than 4% rebound from the previous session as risk appetite improved following the US decision to pause higher tariffs for most countries.

On Wednesday, President Trump reduced import duties to 10% for a 90-day period to support ongoing trade negotiations, easing fears of a full-scale trade war that could trigger a global recession and dampen oil demand.

However, caution remains as China was excluded from the suspension, with its tariff rate surging to 125% effective immediately.

Adding to market concerns, OPEC+ recently agreed to increase production in May, exerting more supply to an already oversupplied market.

Meanwhile, the latest EIA report revealed a larger-than-expected draw in gasoline and distillate inventories, helping to offset a modest rise in crude stockpiles.

data.title: South Korea Briefly Halts Kospi Trading After Sharp Gains
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun

South Korea's stock-market regulator briefly halted trading on the main exchange Thursday morning after relief over President Trump's tariff pause fueled sharp gains.

The Korea Exchange said in a statement that it activated a "sidecar" limit to suspend trading on the Kospi equities market for five minutes from 9:06am local time. Trading has now resumed.

The trading curb was triggered by the Kospi 200 Futures index's surge of more than 5% earlier, the regulator said. The halt is designed to limit excessive market moves.

The Kospi was last 4.7% higher, coming off earlier gains of more than 5%. The upturn follows Trump's announcement of a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for most trading partners that has sparked a broad-based market rally in South Korea, which faces tariffs that could hurt key exports.

Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com

data.title: Asian Markets Power Higher After Trump's About-Face on Tariffs
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires Staff

Asian stock markets powered higher in early trade, tracking overnight gains on Wall Street after President Trump's decision to pause steep tariffs on a swath of countries that had been put into effect just hours before.

Trump on Wednesday said that he would place a 90-day pause on so-called reciprocal rates he had announced a week earlier on nations the administration views as "bad actors" on trade--with the exception of China, which now faces an even higher 125% tariff.

The pause "pulls stocks and the market from the edge of the cliff," Wedbush analysts said in a note. "This was the news we and everyone on the Street was waiting for."

"Now we would expect massive negotiations across the board over the coming months including China being front and center as the biggest wild card," Wedbush added.

Trump's about-face sent the Dow Jones Industrial Average 7.9% higher on Wednesday, its best day since 2020. while the tech-heavy Nasdaq surged 12%, its best day in 24 years.

In early trade Thursday, Japan's Nikkei Stock Average gained 8.2%, South Korea's Kospi Index was up 5.3%, Australia's S&P/ASX 200 jumped almost 6% in early trade before paring gains to 4.9% while New Zealand's NZX-50 was 3.8% higher.

Oil prices were also boosted by Trump's pause as it sparked a risk-on tone across markets, ANZ Research analysts said in a report. The move has also been "exacerbated by traders being forced to cover some bearish option bets and short positions that had been built over the past few days," they added.

Front-month WTI crude oil futures are 1.0% higher at $62.97/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are 0.8% higher at $65.98/bbl.

Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com

data.title: Gold climbs on safe-haven demand as Trump hikes China tariffs
data.description:
Gold prices rose on Thursday as investors turned to the safe-haven asset after U.S. President Donald Trump escalated tariffs on China, the world's top metal consumer, despite his decision to temporarily ease steep duties on several other countries.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Spot gold was up 0.2% at $3,089.17 an ounce, as of 0022 GMT. Bullion scaled to an all-time peak of $3,167.57 on April 3. U.S. gold futures rose 0.8% to $3,104.90.

* In a stunning reversal, Trump on Wednesday decided to temporarily lower the hefty duties he had just imposed on dozens of countries while further ramping up pressure on China.

* Trump said he would raise the tariff on Chinese imports to 125% from the 104% level, escalating a high-stakes confrontation between the world's two largest economies. The two countries have traded tit-for-tat tariff hikes repeatedly over the past week.

* Gold, traditionally seen as a hedge against economic and political uncertainty, often thrives in a low-interest-rate environment.

* According to minutes from the Federal Reserve's latest meeting, policymakers were nearly unanimous last month in warning that the U.S. economy faced risks of higher inflation alongside slower growth, with some noting "difficult tradeoffs" may lie ahead.

* Traders await the U.S. Consumer Price Index data, due later in the day, and the Producer Price Index on Friday for crucial insights into the Fed's rate trajectory.

* Spot silver fell 0.5% to $30.89 an ounce, platinum lost 0.4% to $933.20 and palladium retreated 1% to $922.50.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

0130 China CPI, PPI YY March

0900 Germany Overall Comprehensive Risk Q2

0900 France Overall Comprehensive Risk Q2

0900 UK Overall Comprehensive Risk Q2

1230 US Core CPI MM, SA, Core CPI YY, NSA March

data.title: Taiwan's Taiex Surges 9.2% to 18986.27 in Early Session
Japan Banks' Outstanding Loans Up 2.8% in March
data.description:
Japan's banks and other lenders held combined loans outstanding of 636.7 trillion yen in March, up 2.8% on year, reported the Bank of Japan on Monday.

Major and regional banks held 558.5 trillion yen of loans during the month under review, up 3% on year, according to central bank figures.

Japan's shinkin lenders, a cooperative bank system serving small and medium-sized enterprises and local residents, held 781.8 billion yen of loans in March, up a 1% on year.

In general, bank lending is considered one indicator of the economic health of a nation or region. Bank loans are used to expand or facilitate business operations or acquire properties, or fund consumer outlays. Rising bank loan totals indicate not only demand, but a willingness of banks to extend credit.

Japan's financial institutions held 1,049.1 trillion yen of deposits and certificates of deposit in the first quarter, up 0.9% on year, added the Bank of Japan.

data.title: Santos Chair Seeks to Reassure Investors Amid Uncertain BackdropMarket Talk
data.description: Santos Chair Keith Spence will seek to calm shareholder nerves at the Australian energy company's annual general meeting, following a 15% drop its in stock price this month. In prepared remarks, Spence says he believes Santos's stock is undervalued, and has been for some time, but that the company is strong and resilient and he is optimistic about its future. Still, "the last week has added a new layer of complexity," he says. "The global trading system that was based on building open, competitive markets over many decades following World War Two" has been upended. Santos is up 4.8% at A$5.595 amid a broadbased rally in global stocks after President Trump decided to pause steep tariffs on nearly 100 nations. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Newsmax Made Defamatory Statements Against Voting-Machine Company Dominion, Judge Rules
data.description:
By Isabella Simonetti

A Delaware judge ruled Wednesday that Newsmax made false and defamatory statements against Dominion Voting Systems with on-air claims that the voting-machine company helped rig the 2020 U.S. presidential election.

Delaware Superior Court Judge Eric Davis said Newsmax's statements about Dominion "suggest that Dominion engaged in criminal activity by aiding in election fraud," and that the statements attacked the integrity of Dominion's business.

The judge said that even though the statements were defamatory, Dominion would need to prove damages at trial. He also said he couldn't issue summary judgment on questions about whether Newsmax's statements were made with actual malice. A trial is slated to begin April 28.

A Dominion spokesperson said the company was gratified by the court's ruling.

A Newsmax spokesman said that Newsmax covered the 2020 election dispute fairly and didn't defame Dominion.

"This case represents a serious threat to free speech and a free press and Newsmax will defend itself vigorously at trial," he added.

Newsmax settled a lawsuit last year brought by Smartmatic, another voting-technology company, over false claims that Smartmatic helped rig the 2020 presidential election. It said last month it would pay $40 million in that settlement.

Dominion, meanwhile, settled a defamation lawsuit in 2023 with Fox News over false claims about the 2020 election. Fox agreed to pay $787.5 million in that settlement.

Fox News parent Fox Corp. and The Wall Street Journal parent News Corp share common ownership.

Shares of Newsmax began trading publicly last week, surging in their first days of trading. On Wednesday, shares closed down more than 11% at $34.71, while overall markets rallied.

Write to Isabella Simonetti at isabella.simonetti@wsj.com

data.title: Japan Loan Growth Slows in March
data.description:
The value of loans in Japan increased 2.8% year-on-year in March 2025, slowing from a 3.1% gain in February which was the highest reading in seven months.

The latest figure also missed market expectations of 3.1%.

Outstanding loans held by major, regional, and "shinkin" banks reached 636.7 trillion yen.

Major banks led the growth with a 2.4% increase, followed by regional banks at 3.6%, while "shinkin" banks—which cater to smaller businesses and local communities—saw a modest 1% rise.

data.title: Peet Buys Back Over 51,000 Ordinary Shares
data.description:
Residential real estate developer Peet bought back 51,485 of its ordinary shares for AU$67,976, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company plans to repurchase a total of 24.5 million of its ordinary shares, of which 2.7 million shares are remaining.

data.title: Trump's 90-Day Tariff Pause Not as Helpful as it SoundsMarket Talk
data.description: President Trump's 90-day pause on higher U.S. tariffs for most countries is "not as helpful as it sounds," Citi Research's Andrew Hollenhorst says in a research report. "Pausing reciprocal tariffs excluding China doesn't mean the U.S. economy has avoided a slowdown in growth and rise in inflation," the chief U.S. economist says. The U.S.' 10% baseline tariff, additional 125% tariffs on most Chinese goods, and sector-specific tariffs lift the average U.S. effective tariff rate by around 21 percentage points from its level at the start of 2025, he says. "Uncertainty over trade will persist," Hollenhorst adds. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Malaysia Shares Jump 4.5%, Tracking Wall Street RallyMarket Talk
data.description: Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is 4.5% higher at 1464.19. The index is supported by Wall Street's rally and bargain hunting after recent losses, Apex Securities says in a note. Easing tariff concerns could lift sentiment in the near term and investors may target beaten-down stocks, with attention on coming U.S. inflation data for rate cues, the brokerage reckons. Sectors like technology and banking could lead gains on renewed risk appetite, while the energy sector may benefit from the oil price rebound, it adds. Among gainers, Axiata Group adds 4.6% and Gamuda Bhd. is 10% higher. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Helloworld Agrees to Acquire New Zealand Partner; Shares Rise 5%
data.description:
Helloworld Travel said it agreed to acquire its New Zealand partner Barlow Travel Group, from the travel group's shareholders, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The deal is expected to be earnings accretive starting in the current fiscal year, the filing said.

Shares of the company rose 5% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: iOThree Limited Announces Pricing of Initial Public Offering
data.description:
SINGAPORE, April 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — iOThree Limited ("iOThree" or the "Company"), a leading provider of maritime digital technologies including satellite connectivity and digitalization solutions in Singapore with a focus on facilitating the maritime industry towards digital transformation, today announced the pricing of its initial public offering (the "Offering") of an aggregate of 2,100,000 ordinary shares of par value $0.00625 per share (the “Ordinary Shares”), at a public offering price of US$4.00 per share, of which 1,650,000 Ordinary Shares are offered by the Company and 450,000 Ordinary Shares are offered by the selling shareholders of the Company. The Company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of US$6.6 million from the Offering, before deducting underwriting discounts and other related expenses. The Company will not receive any proceeds from any sale of Ordinary Shares by the selling shareholders. In addition, the Company has granted the underwriters a 45-day option (the “Over-Allotment Option”) to purchase up to 247,500 Ordinary Shares to cover over-allotments at the initial public offering price, less underwriting discounts. The Company's Ordinary Shares are expected to begin trading on April 10, 2025, U.S. Eastern time, on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker symbol "IOTR". The Offering is expected to close on April 11, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.

The Company expects to receive net proceeds from the Offering of approximately US$4.6 million, or approximately US$5.5 million if the Over-Allotment Option is exercised in full, after deducting underwriting discounts and the estimated offering expenses payable by the Company. The Company intends to use the net proceeds for: (i) solution development to expand and enhance its current solution offerings for JARVISS; (ii) obtaining class approval from major maritime organization for its range of digital applications; (iii) marketing and branding, including expanding commercial sales team with dedicated marketing staff, and investing in marketing and promotional activities to further expand its customer base and strengthen its brand; and (iv) working capital and general corporate purposes.

The Offering is being conducted on a firm commitment basis. Eddid Securities USA Inc. is acting as the lead underwriter, with Network 1 Financial Securities, Inc. acting as a co-underwriter for the Offering. Bevilacqua PLLC is acting as U.S. securities counsel to the Company, and Ortoli Rosenstadt LLP is acting as U.S. securities counsel to the lead underwriter in connection with the Offering.

A registration statement on Form F-1 relating to the Offering (File No. 333-276674) was originally filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") on January 24, 2024, and was declared effective by the SEC on December 31, 2024, as further amended by post-effective amendments to such registration statement which was declared effective on March 31, 2025. The Offering is being made only by means of a prospectus, copies of which may be obtained, when available, from: Eddid Securities USA Inc., by standard mail to 40 Wall Street, Suite 1606, New York, NY 10005, by email at ecm@eddidusa.com, or by telephone at (+1) 212-363-6888; or Network 1 Financial Securities, Inc., by standard mail to 2 Bridge Avenue, Suite 241, Red Bank, NJ 07701, by email at adampasholk@netw1.com, or by telephone at (+1) 732-758-9001. Copies of the registration statement can be accessed via the SEC's website at www.sec.gov.

This press release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy the securities described herein, nor shall there be any sale of these securities in any state or jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation, or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or jurisdiction.

About iOThree Limited

iOThree is a leading provider of maritime digital technologies including satellite connectivity and digitalization solutions in Singapore focused on facilitating the maritime industry towards digital transformation. The Company was established to adopt an innovative approach towards the management of solutions accustomed to contemporary needs and drive the digital evolution in the maritime industry.

For more information, please visit the Company's website: https://io3.sg/.

Forward-Looking Statements

All forward-looking statements, expressed or implied, in this release are based only on information currently available to the Company and speak only as of the date on which they are made. Investors can find many (but not all) of these statements by the use of words such as "approximates," "believes," "hopes," "expects," "anticipates," "estimates," "projects," "intends," "plans," "will," "would," "should," "could," "may" or other similar expressions in this release. Except as otherwise required by applicable law, the Company disclaims any duty to publicly update any forward-looking statement to reflect events or circumstances after the date of this press release. These statements are subject to uncertainties and risks, including, but not limited to, the expectation that the Offering will be successfully completed, the uncertainties related to market conditions, and other factors discussed in the "Risk Factors" section of the registration statement for the Offering filed with the SEC. Although the Company believes that the expectations expressed in these forward-looking statements are reasonable, it cannot assure you that such expectations will turn out to be correct, and the Company cautions investors that actual results may differ materially from the anticipated results and encourages investors to review other factors that may affect its future results in the Company's registration statement and other filings with the SEC. Additional factors are discussed in the Company's filings with the SEC, which are available for review at www.sec.gov.

For more information, please contact:

iOThree Limited

Investor Relations: ir@io3.sg

data.title: The Next Logical Step After Quitting Corporate Life? Teaching Himself to Build a House.
data.description:
By Ruth Bloomfield | Photographs by Alice Whitby for WSJ

After almost two decades building a successful corporate career, Elliot Adams was done. Exhausted, stressed and unfulfilled, he made the radical decision to treat his burnout by quitting his white-collar job and turning his hand to hard manual labor.

Starting in 2019, Adams, 46, spent five years building a five-bedroom, barn-conversion home in the English countryside for his young family.

He calculates that his efforts, including managing the construction and learning new skills, from carpentry to steel working, saved more than $380,000.

"The architect explicitly said don't try and do it yourself, it is going to be hard and complicated and you don't have any experience," said Adams. "But it was something I really, really wanted to do. Did I ever think I'd bitten off more than I could chew? No, not really. I know that sounds very arrogant, but if you can't believe in yourself what can you believe in? If I can't do something, then I can learn."

Before taking on the task of building the 6,000-square-foot house, Adams had been a director of communications for a string of tech companies. His DIY skills were modest. His wife, Zoë Fox, 48, worked as a statistician in HIV research, and the couple lived in London with their two daughters, Skyla, now 12, and Lorien, now 10.

The family set up had started to change around the time that Lorien was born, when Zoë made a career pivot. She was offered the chance to run her family's property management and development company and felt that working for herself would be a more family-friendly option than working set hours at a hospital.

Then Adams took on a new role at a luxury cruise company. It was, he now feels, a "career misstep." The job involved a lot of international travel, and he did not enjoy the company's hierarchical ethos either. "I think that I was close to a breakdown," he said. "I was exhausted and frustrated."

In 2017, Adams quit his job. Fox was, she said, anxious but supportive. "I could see he was really unhappy, and I trust him," she said.

For as long as he can remember, Adams has wanted to build a home of his own, following in the footsteps of his parents, who built his childhood home in South Wales. "We grew up on the stories about building the house and it seemed a very romantic thing to do," he said.

Adams used his newfound freedom to travel the country trying to find a building site, as well as taking on some consulting work. He wanted a piece of land which was peaceful, rural, but close enough to London for Fox to get to her office. After a couple of false starts, Adams came across a nearly 2-acre site set in the county of Hampshire, about 60 miles southwest of central London.

The plot was formerly a dairy farm. The main building was a ramshackle old barn. "I would say it was about 18 months to two years from collapse, " said Adams.

There was also a concrete yard, several outbuildings, and a Nissen hut, a semicircular corrugated-steel building built during the World War II and used for military storage.

In August 2018, Adams and Fox sold their house, in the north London suburb of Palmers Green, and used the money to subsidize the purchase of the site for about $1.02 million. They were going to need somewhere to live during the build and they decided it made better financial sense to get a mortgage and buy a roughly $570,000 interim house nearby rather than throw money away renting. Another mortgage helped fund the project.

They then hired architects A R Design Studio, which specializes in building contemporary homes in rural areas, to draw up plans to convert the barn. Meanwhile, Adams invested about $30,000 in a digger and a dump truck, learned how to operate them through trial and error, and then set about breaking up the concrete yard which covered the entire site. All that concrete was then crushed down, and later reused on the site for foundations and floors.

Simply clearing the site, including dismantling a modern steel barn, took almost 18 months.

Adams and Fox got some quotes for the cost of the work. To their horror, the firms they contacted quoted them $1.9 million to convert the barn, not including finishing the interiors or any landscaping. "We just didn't have that much money," said Fox.

By the time that building permits were granted in mid-2019, the couple had decided on a Plan B. Adams had always intended to be very involved in the building work. He decided to really step up and work full time as the project manager and general laborer, hoping to also learn new skills as he went on.

In January 2020, the main work began. Adams's strategy was to hire tradesmen to carry out each task, and then appoint himself as their assistant. This meant he could carry out the menial tasks and learn from the professionals at the same time. "They were all happy to have me looking over their shoulder," he said.

Fox said that she would only be willing to move into the house once it had a working kitchen and at least one bathroom. By Dec. 2022, her conditions were met and the family moved in. Work continued around them until the middle of 2023, but the move allowed them to sell their interim house, for $445,000, which meant they had more money to plow back into the project.

Building the house cost $1.53 million — around $380,000 less than contractors had quoted, said Adams. And this sum included finishing most of the interior, landscaping and work to repair outbuildings. "I don't think there is any way I could have stayed at work and used the money to pay someone for all this, because working in London has its costs and not just the tax you pay," said Adams. "There is the GBP6 latte every day, hiring someone to pick up the girls from school because we are not around to do it.

"Yes it has curtailed my personal 'career,' but it has fast tracked our family's standard of living way beyond what we could have hoped for."

This cost/benefit analysis doesn't factor in the paper profit the project has made. The house has been valued at $5.7 million, said Adams. He says the family has no plans to move on.

"For me it was all about the process — more than the final product," said Adam. "I was covered in clay and mud, and with a big smile on my face. I know every inch of the house — it is an incredible feeling."

Adams is now working on building a one-bedroom annex beside the car port, which he plans to rent out. His hope is that these rental units will provide enough income to allow him to stay at home and continue refining and improving the site over the next few years.

For her part, Fox said she is happy to take over the role of main breadwinner, although Adams has found this part of his new life an adjustment. "Every time I buy something I feel like it is Zoe that is paying for it, and that has been the hardest thing," he said.
